name: Tadalafil restored exercising muscle blood-flow regulation in Becker muscular dystrophy
statement: In men with Becker muscular dystrophy, single-dose tadalafil alleviated functional muscle ischemia and restored normal blood-flow regulation in exercising dystrophic muscle by boosting NO-cGMP signaling, but this disease-specific mechanism does not establish healthy-muscle performance benefit.
population_or_context: Men with Becker muscular dystrophy and exercise-induced muscle ischemia.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Abstract reports randomized crossover design and restoration of exercising muscle blood-flow regulation.

caveats: Physiologic endpoint, not strength or endurance performance.
population_profile: Men with Becker muscular dystrophy; low directness for mostly healthy men age 30 to 50.
tadalafil_exposure: Single tadalafil dose; dose not specified in extracted abstract.
indication_context: Experimental Becker muscular dystrophy muscle perfusion study.
endpoint_measure: Exercise muscle oxygenation and attenuation of reflex sympathetic vasoconstriction.
effect_estimate: Functional muscle ischemia alleviated and normal blood-flow regulation restored in BMD.
safety_findings: Detailed adverse events not extracted from abstract.
directness_to_target_population: Low due to rare muscular dystrophy pathophysiology.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Rare disease, physiologic endpoint, small sample, and abstract-level extraction.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence, but transfer requires dystrophin/nNOS biology that does not apply to healthy muscle.
